meaning: adj.荳榊粋譌ｶ螳懃噪,荳榊粋蟄｣闃ら噪
examples: The wild weather did not deter some people from taking an unseasonable dip in the sea.
He was sweating mightily in his unseasonable suit.
Late in the afternoon, it began to snow in Belgrade - unseasonable weather, but somehow reflecting the mood.
Victims were either swept away by the rising waters or buried in mudslides caused by the unseasonable downpours.
